title: P0 prod regression: v8.3.10.2 shell.js breaks play button (rolled back via hot-patch)
description:
Bosco reported 2026-05-03 ~11:30 CDT that the play button no longer works when clicking from the grid or My Library on prod after v8.3.10.2 deploy. Hot-patched prod by reverting /opt/audiobooks/library/web-v2/js/shell.js to the v8.3.10.1 version (sha1 4df270fae9acedaf64cd60b63822a4a1262a5c5c). Bumped cachebust to 1777826705 across 13 web-v2 files. The broken v8.3.10.2 shell.js (sha1 6019b9e29cd7c88a79038d503531f9cf06dfdf13) was preserved at /opt/audiobooks/library/web-v2/js/shell.js.broken-8.3.10.2 for forensic analysis. The new /api/audiobooks/<id>/chapters endpoint (issue 7bf, server-side) is left in place — it still works correctly (verified returning 74 chapters for Water Mirror Echo), so the regression is purely client-side in shell.js.

**Diff to investigate** (git diff v8.3.10.1 v8.3.10.2 -- library/web-v2/js/shell.js): adds  reset, ,  call at line 463, non-blocking chapters fetch at line 471, and the  helper method at line 696. None of these jump out as a play-blocker on static analysis — fetch is non-blocking,  guards prevent null-deref. **Need browser-based reproduction** on devlib.thebosco.club (which still has v8.3.10.2 shell.js) to capture the actual JS console error or audio.play() rejection reason.

**Hypotheses to investigate**:
1. Cross-frame user-gesture activation lost due to the new fetch() invocation between book-load and audio.play() (line 545-547 comment explicitly warns about this pattern)
2. _applyChapterButtonVisibility called pre-DOM-ready in some new code path
3. An unhandled promise rejection in the chapters fetch chain that propagates to break the surrounding async playBook
4. Some interaction with the streaming-translate.js initialization that wasn't there before

**Required for v8.3.10.3 fix**:
- Identify root cause via browser console capture
- Restore the chapter-skip-buttons-everywhere feature (issue 6ub) without breaking play
- Add a regression test that exercises the play() flow end-to-end (not just structural assertions)
- Roll into v8.3.10.3 staged release
close_reason: Closed

title: ShellPlayer: chapter-skip buttons must work for ALL playback paths, not just streaming-translation
description:
library/web-v2/js/shell.js::ShellPlayer hides #sp-skip-back-chapter / #sp-skip-forward-chapter for the English single-stream path because chapter info wasn't surfaced. With the new GET /api/audiobooks/<id>/chapters endpoint (sister bd issue), the player can fetch chapter boundaries on book load and use them.

**Fix scope**:
1. ShellPlayer::playBook — fetch /api/audiobooks/<id>/chapters, store as this.chapters array (silently no-op on 404)
2. Drop the 'streamingTranslate.isStreaming() OR translatedEntries' visibility guard. NEW logic: show skip buttons whenever this.chapters.length > 0
3. _skipBackChapter — find chapter where start_ms <= currentTime*1000 < end_ms; if (currentTime*1000 - start_ms) > RESTART_THRESHOLD_SEC*1000 → audio.currentTime = start_ms/1000 (restart current chapter); else → audio.currentTime = previous chapter's start_ms/1000 (skip to previous chapter)
4. _skipForwardChapter — audio.currentTime = next chapter's start_ms/1000
5. Hide buttons when chapters API returned empty (genuine 'no chapter info' — keep current 'don't expose non-functional controls' principle)

**Files**:
- library/web-v2/js/shell.js — ShellPlayer.playBook fetch+store, _skipBackChapter / _skipForwardChapter rewrites, visibility logic
- library/tests/test_chapter_nav_wiring.py — extend existing tests: 'buttons visible when chapters.length > 0 regardless of streaming path', 'skip-back from mid-chapter restarts current chapter', 'skip-back near chapter start jumps to previous', 'skip-forward jumps to next chapter start', 'buttons hidden when chapters API returns empty/404'

**Existing v8.3.10.1 streaming-translation path behavior**: PRESERVE. Don't break the streaming MSE jumpToChapter / translatedEntries handling — the new chapter array becomes a third trigger for visibility, doesn't replace the existing two.

**i18n**: the existing data-i18n-title attributes on the buttons are unchanged — works as-is.

Bundle into v8.3.10.2 tweak release (Bosco direction). Depends on the chapters endpoint issue (sister).
close_reason: Closed

title: add /api/audiobooks/<id>/chapters endpoint exposing per-book chapter boundaries
description:
The English single-stream audio path (/stream/<id>) has no client-side chapter info, so the v8.3.10.1 chapter-skip buttons (Audiobook-Manager-9by) are deliberately hidden — making them useless for ~90% of the library which is English audiobooks. Source data IS available: every .opus file has chapter boundaries readable via ffprobe -show_chapters (verified on prod 2026-05-03 — Water Mirror Echo by Jeff Chang has 74 chapters with start_ms/end_ms each in the file). The audiobooks.chapter_count column is also already populated.

**Fix scope**: New endpoint GET /api/audiobooks/<id>/chapters returning JSON {chapters: [{index, start_ms, end_ms, title?}, ...]}. Lazy ffprobe + in-memory cache (small, immutable per file). 404 when file has no chapters (rare — mostly podcasts).

**Files**:
- library/backend/api_modular/audiobooks.py (or new chapters.py blueprint sibling) — new route
- library/backend/utils.py / scanner/metadata_utils.py — ffprobe chapter extractor (probably already exists for the existing chapter_count populator — REUSE)
- library/tests/test_audiobooks_chapters.py — endpoint returns chapters; cache works (2nd hit doesn't re-ffprobe); 404 for chapterless file; permission/auth test mirror existing patterns

**Constraints**: admin_or_localhost auth (matches /api/audiobooks). Cache-Control headers OK for caching since chapter data is immutable per file (max-age=86400). No CHANGELOG/.staged-release/VERSION edits — coordinator handles those.

Bundle into v8.3.10.2 tweak release (Bosco direction). Pairs with frontend issue (separate).
close_reason: Closed

title: library grid shows translated/ chapter artifacts as standalone audiobooks
description: Per-chapter translation artifacts under Library/{Author}/{Book}/translated/ (e.g. "Cat's Cradle.ch000.zh-Hans.opus") are being ingested into the audiobooks table by library/scanner/add_new_audiobooks.py:_collect_audio_files (line ~96-101) and library/scanner/scan_audiobooks.py:_collect_files_by_format (line ~158-165) because they rglob the entire library tree without excluding 'translated/'. The library-grid API (library/backend/api_modular/audiobooks.py::get_audiobooks line ~562 and grouped.py::get_grouped_audiobooks line ~47) reads from the audiobooks table without a defense-in-depth file_path filter, so the phantom rows surface in the UI as bogus library cards. Mirror of Audiobook-Manager-94p (commit 5e41e9ba) which fixed the same root cause in _count_opus_files / _collect_checksum_files. Prod 2026-05-03 had 128 phantom rows out of 1869 canonical (6.4% pollution); cleaned via direct DELETE. Fix surface: (1) exclude 'translated' in f.parts in both scanner _collect functions, (2) add defense-in-depth filter in audiobooks/grouped queries, (3) data-migrations/011_purge_translated_chapter_rows.sh idempotent for operator installs, (4) regression tests in library/tests/test_scanner.py mirroring TestCountOpusFiles. Discovered via Bosco screenshot of prod library.thebosco.club showing Cat's Cradle ch000-ch013 zh-Hans in grid.
close_reason: Closed

title: audiobook-translation-monitor-live hits StartLimit hourly: Burst=5 blocks 30s timer
description:
Discovered on prod 2026-05-02 while verifying the v8.3.10 GPU pod monitoring services (the new translation-monitor-live and translation-monitor-sampler services that watch for stuck/dead/failed claims).

**Symptom:** `audiobook-translation-monitor-live.service` cycles between 'failed (start-limit-hit)' for 4-5 minute stretches and brief recovery to a single successful run, repeating every ~5 minutes. journalctl shows continuous 'Start request repeated too quickly' / 'Failed with result start-limit-hit' lines from systemd.

**Math:**
- Timer: `OnUnitActiveSec=30s` → 2 firings/minute = 10 firings per 5min window
- Service: `StartLimitBurst=5`, `StartLimitIntervalSec=300` → max 5 starts per 5min = 1 start/minute
- Result: timer fills the burst quota in 150s, next 150s of firings are blocked. Live tier runs ~5×/5min instead of intended 10×/5min — 50% miss rate on 'claim reset, retry sweep'.

**Why it matters:** these are the exact services Bosco asked about — the 'monitor for stuck/dead/failed GPU pods' wiring from v8.3.10. They reset stuck `streaming_segments.state='processing'` rows, retry failed segments, and clean up orphaned worker claims. Half of those sweep cycles are silently being dropped.

**Fix (rolled into staged v8.3.10.1):** raise `StartLimitBurst` from 5 to 20 in `systemd/audiobook-translation-monitor-live.service`. 20 starts per 300s = 1 every 15s, 4× headroom over the 30s timer cadence. Still enforces a runaway cap if the service entered a true fail-loop (saturates at 20 in 5min instead of the healthy 10/5min). `StartLimitIntervalSec=300` unchanged.

Sampler tier (`OnUnitActiveSec=5min`, `StartLimitBurst=5`, `Interval=600`) is healthy — only 2 starts per 600s window, well under the 5-burst limit. No change needed.

**Source:** Bosco's question 2026-05-02 'are the new services to watch for stuck, dead, or failed GPU pods working as expected in prod?' — turned out the answer was 'sampler yes, live no.'
close_reason: Fixed in v8.3.10.1: StartLimitBurst raised from 5 to 20 on audiobook-translation-monitor-live.service. Math: 30s timer × 5min window = 10 firings, but Burst=5 only allowed 5 — half the sweeps silently dropped. Bumped Burst gives 4× cadence headroom. 6 regression tests in test_translation_monitor_systemd.py guard the math going forward.

title: Library Collections — fiction/non-fiction/subgenre/era classifications mixed up
description:
Bosco report 2026-05-02: "The Collections are also out of whack. Fiction & non-fiction are getting mixed, subgenres are getting mixed, items are getting classified as the wrong genre and subgenre, Eras isn't working, etc."

Surfaced during the v8.3.10 incident triage. Collections sidebar (`library/web-v2/index.html` → `<aside id="collections-sidebar">`) shows broken filters for FICTION / NONFICTION / SERIES / ERAS / TOPICS in the user-facing library view.

**Investigation needed:**
- Where is genre/subgenre/era classification stored? (audiobooks table columns? a separate mapping table? audiobook_tags?)
- Where is the classification computed? (Scanner pass? Manual admin assignment? AI/heuristic classifier?)
- What's the Eras source — book.publication_year? book.metadata? something derived?
- Why are items getting classified as wrong genre — is the classifier confusing fiction/nonfiction signals, or is the read side joining wrong?
- Is there a recent migration that scrambled the data?

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Fiction collection only contains fiction books, with correct subgenres
- Nonfiction collection only contains nonfiction books, with correct subgenres
- Series collection groups books correctly by series
- Eras collection groups books by literary era / time period correctly
- Topics collection groups by subject correctly

**Source:** Bosco direct report, prod, 2026-05-02. Tracking with the v8.3.10 incident bd issues.
close_reason: Fixed in v8.3.10.1: expanded FICTION/NONFICTION genre sets to cover high-volume raw Audible names (Politics & Social Sciences, Literature & Fiction, etc.). Added categorize_genre keyword fallback. New data-migration 011 backfills audiobook_eras from published_year for the 1,851 books missing era assignments.

title: Conversion Progress shows 306% complete (5,829 in library / 1,901 source files)
description:
Bosco screenshot 2026-05-02 shows the back-office Conversion Progress card displaying:
- SOURCE FILES (.AAXC): 1,901
- IN LIBRARY: 5,829
- COMPLETE %: 306%

Library shows 1,180 audiobooks in the user-facing grid view. The 5,829 "in library" figure suggests the count is grabbing something other than audiobook records — probably individual `.opus` chapter files including streaming-audio segments under `/var/lib/audiobooks/streaming-audio/` or a glob that's pulling chapter-translation files.

**Investigation needed:**
- Locate the 'in library' counter (likely in `library/backend/api_modular/utilities_*.py` or `scripts/library-stats.py`)
- Determine the source query/glob and figure out why it's 5x overcounting
- Repair the count to reflect the actual audiobook book count (1,180 per the library grid)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Conversion Progress card matches reality (≤ 100%)
- Source / library counts reconcile against `audiobooks` table count and AAXC file count
- Counter doesn't pick up streaming/translation/sampler-built artifacts
close_reason: Fixed in v8.3.10.1: _count_opus_files and _collect_checksum_files now exclude translated/ subdirectory artifacts. 6 regression tests added. Conversion Progress card will show accurate book count (1,867 base) instead of inflated total (5,829 incl. chapter translations).

title: user_listening_history rows balloon — every 5s position save creates new row
description:
Diagnosed 2026-05-02 from Bosco's prod screenshot: `A Dirty Job` shows 3,671 listens, `The Long Fall` shows 1,102. Root cause is in `library/backend/api_modular/position_sync.py::_update_listening_history`: every position save (every 5s during active playback) sets `session.ended_at = now`, which makes the next call's `get_open_session` find no open session (because ended_at is no longer NULL) and INSERT a fresh row. Effect: ~12 rows/min × hours of playback = thousands of rows per book.

Math: 3671 rows × 5s/row = ~5h continuous playback recorded as 3671 "listens".

**Code fix** (rolled into staged v8.3.10.1): widen `get_open_session` predicate from `ended_at IS NULL` to `ended_at IS NULL OR ended_at > now - 30 minutes`. New `SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MINUTES` constant. Continuous playback within 30-min windows reuses the same row; 30+ min idle creates a new session.

**Data repair** (also rolled in): one-shot data-migrations script that collapses contiguous history rows (gap < 30 min) into single sessions per (user_id, audiobook_id, session window). Preserves earliest started_at and latest ended_at; sums duration_listened_ms.

Affects: TOP LISTENED stats card on `utilities.html`, total_listens count, active_users count (less affected — DISTINCT user_id collapses).
close_reason: Code fix in v8.3.10.1: get_open_session predicate widened to 30-min idle window with localtime-aware SQL. Stops row explosion. Existing prod data repair tracked as separate follow-up (auth.db SQLCipher-encrypted, needs different tooling than the standard data-migrations framework).

title: iOS Chrome mobile player clipped: cap html height at 100svh
description: v8.3.8.8 bumped --player-height 100→200px to address controls clipped behind iOS Chrome's persistent bottom nav. That treats a symptom: the body itself is sized via var(--app-height, 100dvh) and on iOS Chrome both the JS-set --app-height (visualViewport.height) and the 100dvh fallback include the area behind the bottom toolbar, so the player's bottom rows extend behind the toolbar regardless of player height. Fix: cap html height at 100svh via min(). Plan: shell.css:15 + comment update at 213-230, shell.js:887-906 comment, Playwright iOS-Chrome regression test, CHANGELOG v8.3.8.9, dev → QA → Qing real-device proof → release.
close_reason: Closed

title: agnostic path resolution: stream/download/chapters endpoints must rebase stored file_path under AUDIOBOOKS_LIBRARY
description:
The English single-stream audio path (`/api/stream/<id>` in `library/backend/api_modular/audiobooks.py:1000-1035`) does literal `Path(row["file_path"]).exists()` — no rebasing onto the local install's `AUDIOBOOKS_LIBRARY` env var. This violates production-parity for any operator who ships their DB with file_path values from a different scan environment than the local disk layout. Concrete current failure: dev/QA VMs have full library copies at `/srv/audiobooks/Library/...` but the audiobooks DB rows (mirrored from prod per the production-parity model) carry `/hddRaid1/Audiobooks/Library/...` paths — every English single-stream play returns 404 on dev/QA, breaking the QA-cycle smoke-test for English-content player features. (Translated-audio and streaming-MSE paths use their own DB tables with locally-generated paths and are unaffected.)

The fix is in the code, not the data. The DB rows must remain identical to prod (the production-parity principle); the streaming-side code must transparently resolve to whatever absolute path the local install actually uses.

**Helper sketch** (`library/backend/api_modular/audiobooks.py` or shared utility): `_resolve_local_audio_path(stored_path) -> Path | None`:
1. Try `Path(stored_path)` directly — if exists, return it (identity case, covers prod where DB matches disk).
2. Else extract the relative subpath after the canonical `Library/` directory segment and rebase under `AUDIOBOOKS_LIBRARY` env var (default `/srv/audiobooks/Library`). If the rebased candidate exists, return it.
3. Else return None — caller returns 404.

**Apply at all sites that currently read audiobooks.file_path and open the file**:
- `stream_audiobook` (`/api/stream/<id>`)
- `download_audiobook` (`/api/download/<id>`)
- `_serve_webm` (Safari/iOS WebM remux helper)
- `_ffprobe_chapters` adapter and `get_audiobook_chapters` route (the chapter-list endpoint added in v8.3.10.2 — same problem, also returns empty/404 on dev/QA today; verify with grep)
- Any other call site found by grep that does `Path(<file_path from audiobooks row>).exists()` or `send_file(file_path, ...)`

**REPO MUST REMAIN 100% AGNOSTIC** (per Bosco): no operator-specific path strings anywhere in repo code, comments, tests, or docs. The helper uses only:
- The canonical project convention `Library/` directory segment as the marker
- The operator-supplied `AUDIOBOOKS_LIBRARY` env var
- The opaque stored `file_path` from the DB

Test fixtures use synthetic agnostic paths under pytest `tmp_path` (e.g., `tmp_path / "Library" / "TestAuthor" / "TestBook" / "test.opus"`), never reference `/hddRaid1` or `/srv/audiobooks` or any real-world prefix.

**Regression tests** in new `library/tests/test_audio_path_resolution.py`:
- `test_identity_case_returns_stored_path_when_it_exists`
- `test_rebase_resolves_foreign_prefix_to_local_audiobooks_library`
- `test_returns_none_when_neither_stored_nor_rebased_exists`
- `test_no_directory_traversal_via_dotdot` (defensive — assert `..` segments can't escape AUDIOBOOKS_LIBRARY)
- `test_stream_endpoint_serves_when_db_path_doesnt_exist_but_local_does` (integration via Flask test client)

**Doc**: short subsection in `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` titled "Path resolution: stored paths vs local resolution" explaining the production-parity principle and how the helper enforces it.

**Net effect**:
- Prod: zero behavior change (identity branch returns immediately; never hits the rebase path).
- Dev/QA: English single-stream play, download, chapters endpoint all work because the rebase resolves to /srv/audiobooks/Library/...
- Any future operator install with a different AUDIOBOOKS_LIBRARY root: works without manual DB rewrite.

Bundle into v8.3.10.4 tweak release. Restores the QA-cycle's English-play smoke-test capability that the production-parity DB-mirror model assumed but the literal-path code never honored.
close_reason: Closed

title: header: globe icon should depict Earth with continents; locale dropdown lost theme consistency
description:
From Bosco screenshot 2026-05-03: header has two UI/UX issues:

1. Globe icon: Currently a wireframe globe SVG (library/web-v2/shell.html lines 75-80 — circle + meridian + 2 longitude curves) inside .locale-switcher. User wants it replaced with an icon that depicts Earth (visible continents). Options: (a) Unicode globe emoji with continents, (b) custom SVG of Earth matching the Art Deco gold/dark theme, (c) Font-Awesome fa-earth-americas if FA is available. Match the project's Art Deco palette (var(--deco-gold)).

2. Locale dropdown lost theme consistency: The select id=locale-select in library/web-v2/shell.html lines 81-84 is styled in library/web-v2/css/i18n.css lines 53-65 with appearance:none, transparent bg, gold border. But on Bosco's recent prod view the dropdown is rendering with the default browser native styling (white bg, system font, default arrow) — completely outside the Art Deco theme. May be a rendering regression from a recent CSS change, OR the appearance:none rule may have lost specificity, OR a parent rule may now override. Need to inspect computed styles on devlib in DevTools, fix the CSS, and add a custom dropdown arrow SVG to replace the default (since appearance:none removes it).

Bundle into the v8.3.10.3 staged release alongside the play-button fix (sister bd issue 8mm P0).
close_reason: Closed

title: Top Listened widget counts position-update slices, not real listens; also splits books across stale audiobook_ids
description:
library/backend/api_modular/admin_activity.py::get_activity_stats at line 273-283 builds the Top Listened leaderboard via 'SELECT audiobook_id, MAX(title), COUNT(*) FROM user_listening_history GROUP BY audiobook_id ORDER BY cnt DESC LIMIT 10'. Two compounding bugs:

(1) user_listening_history rows are ~5-second position slices written by the resume-tracking system. 9939/10106 prod rows (98.3%) are 5-60sec long. COUNT(*) of these is 'position-update events', not 'listens'. Prod 2026-05-03 showed 'A Dirty Job: 3671 listens' — actually 3671 slices = ~5h of real listening over 23 days.

(2) audiobook_id is a stale FK — when the library is re-imported, books get new IDs but listening history retains the old IDs. 'A Dirty Job' has TWO audiobook_id values in history (114107 with 3671 slices Mar1-24, 115970 with 1 slice Apr8). The buggy GROUP BY treats them as different books, also possibly hides real top-listened books whose history is split across IDs.

Fix (per Bosco's choice — Option C — Sun May 3): Replace COUNT(*) with SUM(duration_listened_ms)/3600000 (hours), display as 'Xh Ym', collapse stale IDs by GROUP BY title (or join to current audiobooks via title for canonical id). Backend: admin_activity.py top_listened query + total_listens metric. Front-end: library/web-v2/js/utilities.js renderTopList + audit-top-listened tooltip + label, library/web-v2/utilities.html label markup. NO i18n parity needed — admin-only widget per feedback_i18n_scope.md. Bundle into staged v8.3.10.1.
close_reason: Closed

title: hashes not auto-generated for new ingested audiobooks
description: When new books land via library/scanner/add_new_audiobooks.py, the audiobooks.sha256_hash column is left NULL because _run_post_insert_hooks() (lines ~120-142) lost its hash-generation hook in commit a62281369 (2026-01-14 modular utilities_ops refactor). Enrichment, verification, and translation hooks are still wired; hash-generation is the gap. Manual workaround: user clicks Generate Hashes in back office, which fires POST /api/utilities/generate-hashes-async -> generate_hashes.py subprocess. Fix surface: add per-book hash call in _run_post_insert_hooks() targeting only the newly-added audiobook_id (avoid full-library re-scan). DB schema already has sha256_hash TEXT and hash_verified_at TIMESTAMP at schema.sql:55-56 with index idx_audiobooks_sha256. Regression test in library/tests/test_utilities_ops_hashing.py: insert new book via scanner path, assert sha256_hash != NULL. Bundle into staged v8.3.10.1 with the chapter-artifact fix above.
close_reason: Closed

title: Service Status card shows '3/3 RUNNING' but only lists 3 of 9+ audiobook services
description:
Bosco screenshot 2026-05-02 shows the Service Status card on devlib.thebosco.club with header '3/3 RUNNING' and only three services listed: audiobook-converter, audiobook-mover, audiobook-downloader.timer.

But dev VM (and any healthy install) has at least 9 audiobook-* services that should be displayed:
- audiobook-api.service
- audiobook-proxy.service
- audiobook-redirect.service
- audiobook-stream-translate.service
- audiobook-converter.service
- audiobook-mover.service
- audiobook-scheduler.service
- audiobook-translation-monitor-live.timer
- audiobook-translation-monitor-sampler.timer

The '3/3 RUNNING' count is misleading — implies completeness when it's a strict subset. Either (a) expand the card to show all audiobook-* units, or (b) rename the count to clarify scope (e.g. 'Conversion Services 3/3' if intentionally scoped).

Likely the API endpoint `library/backend/api_modular/utilities_system.py` has a hardcoded service list that hasn't been updated as new services were added in v8.3.x.
close_reason: Fixed in v8.3.10.1 commit 4eab46ba — new STATUS_SERVICES roster enumerates all 15 audiobook units; controllable flag flows API→JS; read-only units render dash. 2 new regression tests in TestGetServicesStatus.

title: Conversion Progress shows '0% Calculating...' when SOURCE FILES is 0
description:
On dev VM with no .aaxc source files, Conversion Progress card shows 0% with 'Calculating...' status indefinitely. With zero source files there's nothing to calculate — the card should show '100% Complete' or 'No sources to convert' or hide the progress bar entirely.

Affects any environment without source files (fresh installs, dev VMs, prod-without-Audible-cache).

Fix: in `library/web-v2/js/utilities.js` conversion-progress render path, when source_count==0 show a definitive empty-state message instead of indeterminate calculating.
close_reason: Fixed in v8.3.10.1 commit 4eab46ba — when source_count===0 conversion progress shows definitive empty state (bar=100%, percent=—, rate=—, ETA='No sources to convert').

title: Mobile: Conversion Progress 'IN STAGING' / 'CONVERSION RATE' labels truncated at right edge
description:
Bosco screenshot 2026-05-02 from devlib.thebosco.club shows the Conversion Progress card on the back office utilities view. The 4-column layout (SOURCE FILES / IN LIBRARY / IN STAGING / CONVERSION RATE) overflows the mobile viewport — the third and fourth column labels are clipped to 'IN STAGI' and 'CONVERSI RATE' respectively.

Investigation: `library/web-v2/utilities.html` Conversion Progress card. Likely a fixed-grid layout that doesn't reflow on narrow viewports. Fix candidates: 2-column grid on mobile, or stacked layout, or shorter labels ("STAGED" / "RATE").
close_reason: Fixed in v8.3.10.1 commit 4eab46ba — .conversion-stats reflows to 2x2 grid at ≤480px with overflow-wrap:anywhere on labels.

title: Mobile book cards have huge vertical empty space requiring multi-screen scroll past
description:
On Chrome / Brave / Edge on iOS — Bosco's iPhone and Qing's iPhone — the book card detail view centers the card on screen but leaves several screen-heights of empty space above and below that the user has to scroll past before / after the card is visible. The object is centered, but the surrounding empty space is wrong.

**Symptom (Bosco, 2026-05-02):** "Objects get centered in the screen, but there's a ton of empty space One must scroll past before and/or after the object."

**Investigation needed:**
- Audit all mobile views in `library/web-v2/*.html` for layouts that use `min-height: 100vh` (or `100svh`/`var(--app-height)`) combined with `display: flex; align-items: center;` or `justify-content: center;` on a container whose intrinsic content is shorter than the viewport
- Common culprit: a section that's `min-height: 100vh` flex-centers the card, then sits inside a parent that ALSO has its own height contribution → the page becomes 2× viewport tall with empty padding on both sides of the card
- iOS-specific: Safari/Chrome iOS toolbars resize the visual viewport on scroll, which can interact with `100vh` units to over-allocate space. Project already has a partial fix using `min(100svh, var(--app-height))` (see 2026-04-26 memory entry, recent.md) — verify this is applied everywhere needed
- Likely affected pages: book detail card, login modal, account modal, any 'empty state' page, the player overlay during streaming buffering

**Reproduction (browser tools):**
1. Open `library.thebosco.club` on iOS Chrome / Brave / Edge OR resize desktop Chromium to iPhone-width with iOS user agent
2. Tap a book to open its detail card
3. Try to scroll — observe ~1–3 screen heights of empty space above and/or below the centered card

**Acceptance criteria:**
- No mobile view requires scrolling past empty space to reveal centered content
- Cards / modals are centered visually but the page itself doesn't extend beyond the natural content + sensible margin
- Verified on iOS Chrome (Bosco's primary), Brave (Bosco's), and Chrome iOS (Qing's)
- Desktop layouts unaffected (vertical centering on tall viewports is fine for desktop)
- Existing `min(100svh, var(--app-height))` viewport-height fix preserved or extended

**Source:** Bosco direct report 2026-05-02. Should be tackled as part of the broader mobile-tweaks pass after Audiobook-Manager-9by (chapter nav).
close_reason: Fixed in v8.3.10.1 with two-part fix: (1) hide redundant top-pagination on ≤768px (responsive.css), (2) scroll-lock the book-detail modal when it opens to prevent Brave Android's dynamic URL bar from leaving the position:fixed modal outside the visible viewport. 7 structural regression tests + matching CHANGELOG entry.

title: Add 'Forthcoming' navigation button below 'About'
description:
Bosco's request, 2026-05-02: place a navigation button labelled 'Forthcoming' (or equivalent — verify the existing route's user-facing label) directly below the 'About' button so the forthcoming-releases view is one tap away from any page.

**Investigation needed before implementing:**
- Locate the existing 'About' button in `library/web-v2/` markup (likely in a side menu or top nav).
- Confirm what URL/route the forthcoming view is served at (likely `forthcoming.html` or a query param on the library view).
- Match the button's styling, i18n key pattern, and click handler shape to the existing About button.
- Add curated zh-Hans translation for the new label per `feedback_in_app_docs_i18n_parity.md`.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- New button appears directly below the existing About button on every page that hosts the navigation
- Tapping it navigates to the forthcoming view
- i18n parity (en + curated zh-Hans)
- Styling matches About visually (size, font, spacing, hover/active states)
- Mobile-friendly tap target (≥44pt per Apple HIG)
close_reason: Implemented in v8.3.10.1: 'Forthcoming' nav link added to help.html header-actions row between About and Suggestions, links to #roadmap. i18n parity en + curated zh-Hans (即将推出).

title: Player: chapter-level navigation (prev/next chapter, start/end of chapter)
description:
The mobile and desktop player currently only exposes `±30s` skip buttons and a scrubber. Listeners (Qing in particular, on iOS Chrome) need chapter-level navigation that mirrors the controls of native audiobook apps:

- **Skip back to start of current chapter** — equivalent to `audio.currentTime = chapterStart`. If already within the first ~3 seconds of the chapter, this should jump to the start of the *previous* chapter (standard audiobook UX: tap once = restart, tap again = go back).
- **Skip forward to start of next chapter** — equivalent to `audio.currentTime = nextChapterStart`. Sets up a clean handoff to the next chapter's audio (legacy translatedEntries OR streaming MSE pipeline, whichever applies).
- **Optional: skip to end of current chapter** — useful when the listener wants to rapid-advance and trigger the chapter-end auto-advance flow.

Surfaced during the v8.3.10 prod incident on 2026-05-02 — when Qing got stuck looping ATLWCS chapter 4 (the v8.3.10.1 fix), there was no in-player way for her to jump past the broken state to chapter 5. She had no recovery option short of force-quitting Chrome. Chapter-level controls would have been the user-side workaround.

**Implementation notes (preliminary):**
- Player markup lives in `library/web-v2/shell.html`; player JS in `library/web-v2/js/shell.js` (`ShellPlayer` class).
- Chapter boundaries come from the audiobook's chapter metadata. For legacy translatedEntries playback, each entry is a separate URL/audio file — "prev/next chapter" is a src swap (the existing `ended` handler already does this for next-chapter advance).
- For streaming MSE playback, `streamingTranslate.handleSeek(currentTime)` already exists for scrubber moves. Chapter jumps need to invoke the same `advanceChapter` / start-of-chapter logic, possibly with a new `window.streamingTranslate.jumpToChapter(idx)` entry point.
- Mobile-first: the four buttons must fit in the player bar without crowding ±30s and play/pause. Consider replacing or augmenting ±30s on small viewports.
- Subtitles overlay (`subtitles.js`) needs to follow chapter jumps — already wired for scrubs, should JustWork™ if jumps go through the same code path.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Tap "prev chapter" mid-chapter → player restarts current chapter from t=0
- Tap "prev chapter" within first ~3s of chapter → player jumps to previous chapter
- Tap "next chapter" → player advances to start of next chapter (works for both legacy translatedEntries and streaming MSE paths)
- Position auto-save reflects the jump
- Subtitles re-sync to the new position
- Works on iOS Chrome / Safari / desktop Chromium without breaking gesture-chain auto-play

Source: 2026-05-02 morning conversation with Bosco. Part of a broader "mobile view tweaks" backlog he flagged but hasn't fully enumerated yet — file additional issues as he names them.
close_reason: Implemented in v8.3.10.1: chapter-skip buttons (⏮ / ⏭) wired in shell.html, shell.js, streaming-translate.js with jumpToChapter() public API. 17 structural tests in test_chapter_nav_wiring.py lock the wiring.

title: Subtitle side-panel: stronger active highlight + faster scroll resync after user nudge
description:
Two related UX issues on Qing's iPhone:
1. Active transcript cue too dim — opacity:1 + 18% gold tint background reads as faintly-shaded against the Art Deco dark wood. Hard to find playing line in bright light.
2. Side-panel scroll falls 4 full seconds behind narration after any user scroll (USER_SCROLL_PAUSE_MS = 4000ms in subtitles.js:39). Even an incidental nudge silences narration-follow.

Fix:
- i18n.css: bump .transcript-cue.active background to ~0.35 gold opacity, add brighter text color (cream/near-white #fff5d8), subtle box-shadow glow.
- subtitles.js:39: USER_SCROLL_PAUSE_MS 4000 → 1500.
- subtitles.js:951: scrollIntoView behavior 'smooth' → 'auto' (instant snap; smooth is sluggish on mobile).

Bundle: rolls into staged v8.3.10.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 8ce715aa — i18n.css active-cue background 15%→35% gold, foreground #eddcc4→#fff5d8 cream, +12px gold glow box-shadow. Same on bilingual variant. subtitles.js USER_SCROLL_PAUSE_MS 4000→1500, scrollIntoView behavior smooth→auto. Rolled into staged v8.3.10. Visual UAT pending.

title: Chinese streaming: pre-buffer next chapter so iOS auto-advance survives gesture chain
description:
Streaming-translate chapter end on iOS Chrome (Qing) silently fails to auto-advance because the post-ended chain (POST /translate/stream → bitmap fetch → MSE rebuild → sourceopen → play()) crosses multiple async ticks and iOS rejects the autoplay continuation. Manual play tap is required at every chapter end — kills 'getting lost in the story' UX.

English (cached) auto-advance works because shell.js:151 does a single sync src swap then await audio.play() — gesture chain preserved.

Fix: in streaming-translate.js, listen to timeupdate on the audio element. When (audio.duration - audio.currentTime) < ~10s AND chapter+1 < totalChapters AND we haven't already pre-buffered, eagerly POST /translate/stream for the next chapter and pre-fetch its bitmap into a 'nextChapterPreload' state. On the actual ended event, advanceChapter uses the preloaded data instead of fetching — MSE rebuild + play() runs in the same task as ended, gesture chain stays intact.

Bundle: rolls into staged v8.3.10 per feedback_no_auto_patch_bump.md.
close_reason: Fixed in commit cd92fe47 — maybePreloadNextChapter runs on timeupdate, eagerly POSTs /translate/stream for chapter+1 when 10s remain. advanceChapter takes fast path on ended (no network round-trip), play() inside iOS gesture chain. Slow-path fallback preserves prior behavior on chapters shorter than 10s. Rolled into staged v8.3.10. Visual UAT pending on Qing's iPhone via QA.

title: audiobook-shutdown-saver runs as root but audiobook-save-staging requires audiobooks user (staging never saved on shutdown)
description:
audiobook-shutdown-saver.service has `User=root` (systemd/audiobook-shutdown-saver.service:9) but `/usr/local/bin/audiobook-save-staging` enforces `audiobooks` user and exits 1 if invoked as root:

  `error: audiobook-save-staging must run as the audiobooks user.`
  `current:  root (uid 0)`
  `required: audiobooks`

Result: every shutdown attempt to save tmpfs staging FAILS. Discovered via journalctl during v8.3.9 prod upgrade verification (Audiobook-Manager-hp2 investigation).

Implication: audiobook tmpfs staging is NOT being persisted on clean shutdown despite the unit existing for that purpose. Any in-flight conversions in /tmp/audiobook-staging are lost on reboot.

**Fix options** (need investigation to pick):
1. Change service `User=root` to `User=audiobooks` (correct but may break if save-staging needs root for some path)
2. Change `audiobook-save-staging` to allow root execution (relaxes the permission model)
3. Wrap with `sudo -u audiobooks` in ExecStart (ugly but minimal)

Probably (1) — the script's complaint is that root bypasses the audiobooks:audiobooks ownership model. If the unit runs as audiobooks and the target dirs are owned by audiobooks, no privilege escalation needed.

Verify on dev VM first.
notes: Discovered 2026-04-27 during v8.3.9 prod upgrade verification. journalctl shows the same FAILURE pattern repeating at every upgrade-helper invocation (1983116, 1983663). audiobook-shutdown-saver.service is WantedBy=halt.target reboot.target shutdown.target so it activates only at shutdown — meaning this bug is dormant during normal operation but every reboot loses staged work.
close_reason: Fixed in commit (this commit) — User=root → User=audiobooks in systemd/audiobook-shutdown-saver.service; smoke-tested on prod (unit now runs active 0/SUCCESS instead of failing); 3 new regression tests in test_shutdown_saver_user.py; CHANGELOG [Unreleased].

title: upgrade.sh: 'Started ...' phase doesn't start newly-added timers/services (silent gap)
description:
upgrade.sh enables new units (line 956: `systemctl enable`) but the post-upgrade 'Started ...' phase only restarts services that were running before the upgrade. Result: new timers added in a release sit `enabled` but `inactive (dead)` until the next reboot.

Discovered on prod 2026-04-27 16:30 CDT after v8.3.8.14 → v8.3.9 upgrade — both `audiobook-translation-monitor-live.timer` and `audiobook-translation-monitor-sampler.timer` were enabled but never started; `systemctl list-timers` returned 0 matches. Fixed manually via `sudo systemctl start <timer>`.

Same gap affected v8.3.0 `audiobook-stream-translate.service` (memory `feedback_upgrade_consistency_enforcement.md`) — the 'belt-and-suspenders' line at upgrade.sh:963 only enables, doesn't start.

**Fix**: change `systemctl enable "$unit"` to `systemctl enable --now "$unit"` in the standalone_units loop (upgrade.sh:954-959), and the same for line 963 (stream-translate). Also add new units to the post-upgrade restart loop in scripts/audiobook-start so newly-introduced units are actively brought up rather than relying on the next reboot.

**Regression guard**: `/test` Phase V should assert that every timer in /etc/systemd/system/audiobook-*.timer is `active` after a fresh install + upgrade cycle, not just `enabled`.
notes:
Implemented in commits TBD on main:

- upgrade.sh::enable_new_services: replaced bare `systemctl enable` calls (lines 941, 956, 963) with new `_enable_unit_smart` helper that parses [Install] WantedBy= and applies `enable --now` for runtime units, plain `enable` for shutdown-only
- install.sh::do_system_install: same helper inline-duplicated (must stay in sync; both scripts bootstrap before shared shell library is on target)
- install.sh::do_user_install: blanket `enable --now` (only api+web; no shutdown-only units in user-mode set)
- uninstall.sh: reviewed, no change — dynamic-discovery + stop+disable pattern is correct
- CHANGELOG [Unreleased] updated

Two follow-ups filed: shutdown-saver User=root bug (separate); /test Phase V regression guard (claude-test-skill repo).

Verified on real units: helper correctly classifies all 4 monitor timers + stream-translate + scheduler as enable_now, shutdown-saver as enable_only. shellcheck clean.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 4dc3993 — _enable_unit_smart helper applied to upgrade.sh + install.sh (system + user mode); uninstall.sh reviewed, no change needed; CHANGELOG [Unreleased] updated; follow-ups c8l + cf2 filed for the discovered shutdown-saver root-user bug and /test Phase V regression guard.

title: Auto-clear stale upgrade-status lock when helper crashes mid-upgrade
description:
When audiobook-upgrade-helper.service crashes mid-upgrade, /var/lib/audiobooks/.control/upgrade-status is left with running:true and stage:starting_services (or similar mid-flight stage). All subsequent /api/utilities/check-upgrade calls then return HTTP 400 'An operation is already in progress' from library/backend/api_modular/utilities_system.py::_check_not_running, blocking both the in-app upgrade UI and the CLI helper.

Today's incident (2026-04-27): user clicked 'Check for Updates' to upgrade prod from v8.3.8.14 to v8.3.9. UI returned 'Failed to check for updates: An operation is already in progress'. The status file was carrying a 21-hour-old running:true from a 2026-04-26 v8.3.8.13 upgrade that crashed during 'Restoring services after upgrade failure' — the smoke-probe-during-managed-services bug that v8.3.8.14 itself fixed. v8.3.8.14 prevented future occurrences but didn't clean up the existing stale lock.
design:
Mirror the structural pattern of library/translation_monitor/probe.py::reset_stuck_live_claims — same problem class (worker crashes mid-operation leaves a running:true flag), same fix shape (timeout-gated reset).

Approach: in _read_status() (utilities_system.py:95) or as a wrapper called from _check_not_running() (line 178), detect staleness via TWO conditions (defense-in-depth, both required):

1. Status file mtime older than UPGRADE_HELPER_STALE_TIMEOUT_SEC (suggested default: 1800 = 30 min — generous because legitimate upgrades can take 5-10 min, and we don't want to clear a slow-but-healthy upgrade).

2. systemctl is-active audiobook-upgrade-helper.service returns 'inactive' or 'failed'.

When both fire, auto-clear: HELPER_STATUS_FILE.write_text('') and emit a structured log line (or write to a new translation_monitor_events-style audit table). This is the same idempotency pattern used by reset_stuck_live_claims — running it twice with no intervening helper activity is a no-op.

Two-condition gate prevents a false-positive when the helper is genuinely working through a long upgrade (file is being actively written even if the timestamp wraps a checkpoint).
acceptance_criteria:
1. A status file with running:true AND mtime > UPGRADE_HELPER_STALE_TIMEOUT_SEC AND helper service inactive auto-clears on next /api/utilities/check-upgrade call (or on a periodic timer).
2. A status file with running:true AND mtime fresh (<30 min) is left alone (don't break legitimate slow upgrades).
3. A status file with running:true AND helper service active is left alone (don't break legitimate in-progress upgrades).
4. Auto-clear emits a log line so operators can see 'this happened' without it being silent.
5. Test exercises all 4 cases above.
notes:
Filed 2026-04-27 from prod incident response. User on v8.3.8.14 trying to upgrade to v8.3.9 hit blocked-by-stale-lock from yesterday's v8.3.8.13 attempt.

Bundle target: v8.3.10 patch. CHANGELOG entry under ### Added: 'Defensive auto-clear of stale upgrade-helper lock to prevent 'operation in progress' false-blocks after a crashed upgrade.' Equivalent in scope to translation_monitor's claim-reset for streaming_segments.

Threshold rationale: 30 min covers normal upgrades (5-10 min) plus 3x safety margin. Could be tunable via /etc/audiobooks/audiobooks.conf if operators want stricter/looser windows.
close_reason: Fixed in commit bcde439 — _clear_stale_lock_if_safe + _helper_service_active helpers in utilities_system.py with two-condition gate (mtime > 30min AND helper service inactive); fails closed on systemctl probe failure; wired into _check_not_running; 12 new unit tests cover all branches (TestStaleLockAutoClear + TestHelperServiceActive); full utilities_system suite (119 tests) passes — no regression; CHANGELOG [Unreleased] updated under Added.

title: upgrade.sh sub-sudo calls fail in non-TTY contexts (require terminal even with NOPASSWD)
description: During prod deploy of v8.3.10.1 (2026-05-03), running ./upgrade.sh as a non-root user with NOPASSWD sudo failed with 'sudo: a terminal is required to read the password' on the internal sudo call to write to /opt/audiobooks. User had NOPASSWD: ALL per sudoers, and 'sudo -n true' worked from the same shell — confirming sudo cache was valid and non-interactive sudo is permitted. The failure happens specifically when upgrade.sh's internal sudo invocation runs without a controlling terminal (likely from a function with redirected stdin/stdout, or via a here-doc/pipe). Workaround: invoke 'sudo ./upgrade.sh ...' to elevate the entire script — sub-sudo calls then run as root and don't need to elevate. Proper fix: identify the internal sudo call site in upgrade.sh and either (a) add '-n' flag explicitly to fail-fast in non-interactive contexts with a clearer error, or (b) detect the situation upfront and recommend running as root. Possibly also document in TROUBLESHOOTING.md. Bundle into v8.3.11+.

title: add admin Refresh button: purge Cloudflare cache + clear browser cache + hard reload
description:
On the main library page (per Bosco screenshot 2026-05-03), add a button that performs a full cache purge:

1. **Cloudflare cache**: New admin-only endpoint POST /api/admin/cache/purge that calls Cloudflare's purge_cache API for the deployment's hostname. Cloudflare token already exists in operator config (reference_cloudflare_tunnels.md). Provider-agnostic: gate on whether CF token is configured; no-op gracefully if not (per feedback_provider_agnostic_repo_docs.md — repo describes CDN abstraction, named provider is operator recipe).

2. **Browser cache**: Front-end button handler — caches.delete() for any service-worker caches, plus reload via location.replace(URL + '?_cb=' + Date.now()) to force a cache-bypassing navigation. (location.reload(true) is deprecated; cache-buster query param is the reliable cross-browser fallback.)

3. **Visible UI**: Per screenshot the existing yellow 'Refresh' button next to 'Recently Acquired' dropdown is a candidate location for either replacement or a sibling. Decide during design — the existing Refresh likely just re-runs the library query, so the new button needs a distinct affordance ('Hard Refresh', 'Clear & Reload') and should probably live in the user menu or back-office, not the main library bar (admin-only operation).

4. **Auth scope**: admin_required (uses Cloudflare API). Anonymous users should not see the button.

5. **Error handling**: Cloudflare purge can fail (rate limit, token expired). Show toast on success/failure. Always proceed with browser-cache clear + hard reload even if CF purge fails (best-effort cleanup of what's locally cleanable).

**Tests**:
- API: assert /api/admin/cache/purge requires admin, returns 200 on success with mocked CF response, returns 502 on CF failure with message.
- Front-end: button-click handler calls API then navigates with cache-buster.

**Why next release (v8.3.11+)**: Non-trivial feature requiring new external API integration (Cloudflare), new admin-only endpoint, secret handling. Patch release v8.3.10.1 is surgical bug fixes only — adding a feature here violates the patch-vs-feature scope distinction.

Deferred from 2026-05-03 session.

title: architecture: replace ad-hoc rglob collectors + hardcoded post-insert hooks with canonical iterator + self-registration
description:
Both Audiobook-Manager-2sw (chapter-artifact pollution) and Audiobook-Manager-f5e (missing hash hook) are symptoms of two by-vigilance patterns:

1. **rglob collectors with per-call exclusions**: 4+ independent rglob('*.opus') call sites (library/scanner/add_new_audiobooks.py:_collect_audio_files, library/scanner/scan_audiobooks.py:_collect_files_by_format, library/backend/api_modular/utilities_conversion.py:_count_opus_files, library/backend/api_modular/utilities_ops/hashing.py:_collect_checksum_files) each need 'translated/' and '.cover.opus' exclusions. Forgetting one = silent failure (94p, 2sw). Fix: introduce iter_canonical_audiobook_files(library_dir) as the single authoritative iterator, refactor all consumers to use it. Optional nominal type (NewType('CanonicalAudiobookPath', Path)) for static safety.

2. **Hardcoded post-insert hook fan-out**: library/scanner/add_new_audiobooks.py::_run_post_insert_hooks (lines ~120-142) hardcodes calls to enrich_fn, verify_fn, enqueue_book_all_locales. Adding a new hook = remembering to edit this function. Modular utilities_ops refactor (commit a62281369, 2026-01-14) lost the hash-generation hook — silent regression for ~3.5 months. Fix: replace with a self-registration pattern (register_post_insert decorator + iterate registry). Each feature module registers its own hook at import time. Add a regression test that asserts each registered hook fires on a synthetic insert.

3. **Strongest backstop for the specific hash case**: Add sha256_hash NOT NULL CHECK constraint to audiobooks schema after a backfill migration. Lets the DB enforce the contract — insert without hash fails loudly at write-time, not silently as NULL.

Do NOT bundle into v8.3.10.1 — that release is patch-level surface fixes. Target v8.3.11 or v8.4.0. Cross-cutting refactor needs proper QA cycle. File created 2026-05-03 during diagnosis of 2sw + f5e + 5gf + ptj.

title: /api/user/new-books returns no Cache-Control header — marquee shows stale titles after DB cleanup
description: library/backend/api_modular/user_state.py::get_new_books at line 405-462 (route /api/user/new-books) returns a JSON response with NO Cache-Control header. Browsers and intermediate caches may apply heuristic caching to the response. After the chapter-artifact DB cleanup (2sw) the marquee continued to show phantom .ch0NN.zh-Hans titles in the user's open tab because the front-end (library/web-v2/js/marquee.js initMarquee, single fetch on page load) had cached the pre-DELETE response in memory and the browser may also have cached the HTTP response. Hard-refresh cleared it but that should not be necessary. Fix: add Cache-Control: no-store, must-revalidate to the response so the marquee endpoint is always fetched live. Also relevant for the dismiss-state semantics — the response is per-user and should never be cached anywhere. Bundle into staged v8.3.10.1.
close_reason: Closed

title: Mobile: 'Index file not found' path truncated at right edge in Find Duplicates
description:
Find Duplicates view shows error 'Index file not found: /srv/audiobooks/.index/library_checks...' with the path truncated at viewport right edge. Same issue with 'source_check...' truncation. Path should word-break or be displayed in monospace with horizontal scroll on mobile.

`library/web-v2/utilities.html` Find Duplicates section, 'Index file not found' message rendering. Add `word-break: break-all` or `overflow-wrap: anywhere` for path strings on mobile.
close_reason: Fixed in v8.3.10.1 commit 4eab46ba — .duplicates-list .placeholder-text now wraps long paths via overflow-wrap:anywhere/word-break:break-word at ≤480px.

title: upgrade.sh: api start-limit-hit race during in-flight permission normalization
description:
During v8.3.9 → v8.3.10 native upgrade on QA (192.168.122.63), the post-upgrade smoke probe failed with 'API health endpoint unreachable at http://127.0.0.1:5001/api/system/health'. journalctl on QA showed the audiobook-api.service hit StartLimit (default 5 starts in 10 s) immediately after rsync, with each attempt failing at 'Changing to the requested working directory failed: Permission denied' at /opt/audiobooks/library/backend.

Recovery: `sudo systemctl reset-failed audiobook-api.service && sudo systemctl start audiobook-api.service` succeeded immediately. The directory permissions on disk look correct after the upgrade settled (drwxr-xr-x audiobooks:audiobooks on /opt/audiobooks/library/backend) — the failure was during a race window inside upgrade.sh where api restart happened before perms were normalized.

This means: every native upgrade has a small chance of leaving api in start-limit-hit state, requiring manual reset-failed + start. Most upgrades probably succeed (the smoke probe didn't catch this on prior v8.3.9 deploys), so the race window is narrow.

Hypothesis: upgrade.sh's services_start phase runs BEFORE verify_installation_permissions completes, or the sequence within verify_installation_permissions doesn't normalize WorkingDirectory permissions before the service-start cycle. Need to:

1. Trace upgrade.sh's order: rsync → enable_new_services → services_start → verify_installation_permissions (or whatever the actual sequence is)
2. Confirm perms-normalize happens BEFORE services_start
3. If race exists, either reorder OR add a per-service reset-failed-and-retry loop after perms-normalize

Tangentially related: gunicorn logs '[ERROR] Control server error: [Errno 30] Read-only file system' on every QA api start — benign (control socket path) but should be looked at separately if the systemd sandboxing constraints have drifted.
notes: Filed 2026-04-27 from v8.3.10 QA upgrade. Recovery procedure for operators encountering this: sudo systemctl reset-failed audiobook-api.service && sudo systemctl start audiobook-api.service. Bundle target: v8.3.11 patch — not blocking v8.3.10 since the upgrade DID complete and the api IS running, just needed manual nudge.
close_reason: Fixed in this commit. Two-part fix: (1) moved verify_installation_permissions before enable_new_services in do_upgrade, eliminating the perm race exposed by the hp2 'enable --now' change; (2) added defensive reset-failed-and-retry-once loop in _enable_unit_smart (both upgrade.sh and install.sh). Verified on QA — smoke probe ALL CHECKS PASSED on re-upgrade, api came up clean in <2s with zero CHDIR/StartLimit hits in journal. Rolled into staged v8.3.10.

title: /test Phase V should assert all enabled audiobook-*.timer units are 'active' post-upgrade, not just 'enabled'
description:
Regression guard for Audiobook-Manager-hp2. Phase V currently exercises the install→upgrade→deploy→verify lifecycle, but doesn't differentiate between `is-enabled` and `is-active`. The hp2 bug class (enable without --now) silently passed Phase V because the enable state was correct.

Add an assertion: after a fresh install OR upgrade, every `/etc/systemd/system/audiobook-*.timer` whose unit file is enabled MUST have `systemctl is-active <timer>` returning `active`. Same assertion for any non-shutdown-only `audiobook-*.service` that's enabled.

The shutdown-only carve-out (audiobook-shutdown-saver) is detected the same way the install/upgrade fix detects it: parse `[Install] WantedBy=` from the unit file, treat units WantedBy ONLY halt/reboot/shutdown.target as exempt from the active-after-install assertion.

Lives in claude-test-skill repo: skills/test-phases/phase-V-vm-lifecycle.md (or wherever the lifecycle assertions live).
close_reason: Fixed in claude-test-skill commit 990f34f (phase-10b assertion logic) + Audiobook-Manager commit (manifest opt-in + CHANGELOG). The assertion auto-exempts shutdown-only units. Closes the regression-guard sibling of Audiobook-Manager-hp2.

title: Wire ESLint flat config into web-v2 build
description: Phase 6 added library/web-v2/eslint.config.js (v8.3.9). ESLint 10 won't run cleanly because /hddRaid1/ClaudeCodeProjects/package.json is malformed and npx looks parent-ward. Options: (a) add a local package.json with type=module + devDeps, (b) move config to project root, (c) use eslint --resolve-plugins-relative-to. Pick one and seed CI lint job.
notes:
Bundle target: v8.3.10 patch (public layer only; local layer never ships).

Two layers (per public-vs-local split discussed in /test audit session):

PUBLIC LAYER (ships in v8.3.10 release):
- Add CI lint job in .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs eslint against library/web-v2/eslint.config.js
- CHANGELOG: ### Changed — 'CI now runs ESLint against library/web-v2/js/ using the v8.3.9 flat config'
- This is the part outside operators care about — when they clone the repo and run CI, the lint job runs.

LOCAL LAYER (never ships, stays in this bd issue):
- Resolve npx walking up to /hddRaid1/ClaudeCodeProjects/package.json (malformed parent dir on this dev workstation).
- Three options listed in description; pick one:
  (a) library/web-v2/package.json with type=module + devDeps  → adds node_modules surface but most idiomatic
  (b) move eslint.config.js to project root  → keeps lint visible at top level, slight pollution
  (c) eslint --resolve-plugins-relative-to library/web-v2 in CI  → no source-tree change, CI-only

Filed from /test audit on v8.3.9, Phase 6 follow-up.
